name: SmolLM3 3B (Think)
archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer with GQA + NoPE (selective RoPE removal) + intra-document masking
note: Default extended-thinking mode (/think); open 3B multilingual dual-mode reasoner with 128K context, trained on 11.2T tokens.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, extended-thinking mode. From the official model card's 'Extended Thinking' evaluation table (matches the HF blog post's chart).

BFCL (function/tool calling), 'Extended Thinking' evaluation table row (88.8 for SmolLM3-3B, tied with Qwen3-1.7B). No BFCL version is specified on the card, so mapped to unversioned 'bfcl' rather than 'bfcl-v3'. Not previously tracked for this variant.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real 2025-07-08 Hugging Face release, Apache-2.0, 3.08B params, GQA+NoPE decoder trained on 11.2T tokens with 64K native / 128K YaRN-extended context — all verified against the official HF blog. Both benchmark rows (AIME 2025 reasoning-mode 36.7%, LiveCodeBench reasoning-mode 30.0%) confirmed verbatim on that same blog — no changes needed.

2026-07-16: Re-checked the flagged bfcl=88.8 comparability finding by pulling the raw model-card README directly (https://huggingface.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B/raw/main/README.md). Both the 'No Extended Thinking' table ('Tool Calling | BFCL | 92.3 | ... ') and the 'Extended Thinking' table ('Tool Calling | BFCL | 88.8 | 88.8 | 95.5') label the metric simply 'BFCL' with no v2/v3/v4 designation anywhere on the card; evaluations are run via HF's own lighteval harness, not the official Gorilla BFCL codebase, so there's no basis to assign a specific version. The existing entry's slug ('bfcl', unversioned) and its note are already correct -- the flagged finding is a negation-blind audit-tool false positive against a note that already explains the mapping (same known class of false positive documented in the 2026-07-14 completeness/accuracy audit and in the dataset-audit-2026-07 memory). No data changed.
